title: STARLINK-1234 vs COSMOS 1408 DEB (Active/Debris)
description: An active Starlink satellite must perform a unilateral avoidance maneuver because the opposing object is uncontrolled Russian military space debris.

agentA_proposals.justification: Standard operational satellite. Proposing a clean prograde burn of 0.31 m/s to raise apogee, shifting TCA crossing time and increasing miss distance to 47.3 km.
Standard operational satellite. Proposing a clean retrograde burn of 0.45 m/s to lower perigee, allowing the threat to pass overhead safely.
Cross-track normal burn to adjust orbital inclination slightly. Highly fuel-intensive but effective.

agentB_proposals.justification: COSMOS 1408 DEB is a dead debris object. It has no propulsion capabilities and cannot perform maneuvers.

satA_action.justification: Standard operational satellite. Proposing a clean prograde burn of 0.31 m/s to raise apogee, shifting TCA crossing time and increasing miss distance to 47.3 km.

brief:
CONJUNCTION ALERT — HIGH RISK
Objects: STARLINK-1234 and COSMOS 1408 DEB
TCA: 2026-06-09T14:58:47.736343+00:00
Current Miss Distance: 2.539 km — BELOW SAFE THRESHOLD

Recommended Action: STARLINK-1234 will execute an avoidance maneuver; COSMOS 1408 DEB will hold its current trajectory.
Maneuver Details: STARLINK-1234: prograde burn (0.31 m/s) | Fuel Cost: 8.0% of reserve
New Miss Distance: 47.3 km — SAFE

Maneuver Justification:
The automated collision avoidance system has calculated an optimal avoidance strategy. Standard operational satellite. Proposing a clean prograde burn of 0.31 m/s to raise apogee, shifting TCA crossing time and increasing miss distance to 47.3 km. Since COSMOS 1408 DEB is uncontrolled debris, it cannot perform maneuvers. This will increase the closest approach miss distance from 2.539 km to 47.3 km, which is well above the safety threshold of 10.0 km, minimizing the probability of collision while conserving spacecraft fuel reserves.
